headline: Indonesian Conservationist Wins Green Oscar for Forest Defense
meta_description: Conservationist Farwiza Farhan wins top environmental award for grassroots efforts halting dam projects and protecting orangutan forests in Indonesia.
summary_short: Indonesian conservationist Farwiza Farhan wins the "Green Oscars" for her groundbreaking work protecting forests and orangutan habitats from mega-dam projects.
body:
Farwiza Farhan, an Indonesian conservationist, has received the top award at the "Green Oscars"—a global recognition of environmental leadership and achievement. Farhan's grassroots team has achieved remarkable victories, including halting a major dam project and protecting vast forest areas that serve as critical habitat for orangutans and countless other species.

Her work demonstrates the power of dedicated local action in the face of large-scale development pressures. By combining scientific knowledge with deep community engagement, Farhan and her team have shown that it is possible to defend some of Earth's most biodiverse regions from destructive projects.

The award underscores the vital role that grassroots conservationists play in the global fight to protect forests and wildlife. Farhan's success in halting a mega-dam and preserving critical habitat sends a powerful message: determined local leadership can achieve transformative environmental victories.
